prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "单腿臀桥保持" (single-leg glute bridge hold) exercise, side view, facing right. Person posture: Athletic person holding a single-leg glute bridge: lying supine with upper back, head, neck, and arms flat on the ground; right foot flat on the ground with right knee bent at 90 degrees (this is the supporting leg); left leg fully extended straight forward parallel to the floor at the same height as the right thigh, so that left foot, hips, and right shoulder form one straight line; arms resting flat at the sides with palms down; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: gluteus maximus and hamstring group of the supporting (right) leg. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: core muscles and erector spinae and gluteus medius for hip stability. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "臀大肌" pointing to the gluteus maximus of the supporting leg; "腘绳肌" pointing to the hamstring group of the supporting leg; "核心 / 竖脊肌" pointing to the core and erector spinae area. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
